target.name: AlixLabs
target.founder: Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, Amin Karimi

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
AlixLabs is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Jonas Sundqvist, Dmitry Suyatin, and Amin Karimi, having raised €15M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given AlixLabs' private status and undisclosed revenue. The 4-company cohort yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.2x across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, a standard limitation in private semiconductor valuations.

The analysis supports a range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on the peer median of $1.5B. This narrow band reflects the homogeneity of the peer set but carries material uncertainty. Key sensitivities include the sustainability of current valuation levels within the frontier-chip segment, AlixLabs' product-market differentiation relative to the comparison set, and capital efficiency metrics relative to peers—data points not available for this analysis.
